info.title: Map all routes and links (@explore subagent)

parts.text:
I need to understand the user flow and navigation structure of a Qwik application. 
Please explore the `src/routes/` directory thoroughly. 
For each route file (like `.tsx` or `.mdx`), identify:
1. The route path.
2. Any `<Link>` components or `navigate` calls to other routes.
3. What the page appears to be for (editor, settings, etc.).
Also, do a broad search across the whole `src/` directory for any `cursor: default` CSS rules or classes that might remove pointer cursors.
Return a concise summary of the navigation graph and any suspicious cursor rules.

The user wants me to explore the `src/routes/` directory of what appears to be a Qwik application. I need to:
1. Find all route files (`.tsx`, `.mdx`) in `src/routes/`
2. For each route, identify the path, Link components/navigate calls, and page purpose
3. Search the whole `src/` directory for `cursor: default` CSS rules
4. Return a concise summary

Let me start by listing the routes directory and finding files.

Here is the concise summary.

### Navigation Graph

| Route | Page Purpose | Outgoing Links / Navigations |
|:-----|:------------|:-----------------------------|
| `/` (`src/routes/index.tsx`) | **Landing / Onboarding / Workspace** — The main app shell. It conditionally renders a landing page, an onboarding interview, a brief refinery, or the full editor workspace with a left sidebar (folios) and a right editorial board panel. | `<Link href="/personas">` (Room of Editors), `<Link href="/library">` (The Library), `<Link href="/rubric">` (Galley Proof) |
| `/p/[slug]` (`src/routes/p/[slug]/index.tsx`) | **Public Reader** — A static, auth-free page for reading a published piece by slug. | `<Link href="https://twyne.love">Twyne →</Link>` (external) |
| `/rubric` (`src/routes/rubric/index.tsx`) | **Galley Proof** — Displays the full rubric breakdown, grades, judge verdicts, and the "brutal curve". | `<Link href="/">← Back to desk</Link>` (×2) |
| `/personas` (`src/routes/personas/index.tsx`) | **Room of Editors** — CRUD interface for managing editorial personas. | `<Link href="/">← Back to desk</Link>` |
| `/library` (`src/routes/library/index.tsx`) | **The Library** — A grid view of all local folios (drafts, notes, outlines). | `<Link href="/">← Back to desk</Link>`; also uses `useNavigate()` programmatically to go to `/` when a folio card is clicked. |

**Navigation flow:**
- The root `/` is the hub. All other internal routes (`/personas`, `/library`, `/rubric`) link back to `/`.
- `/library` uses `nav("/")` to return the user to the editor when a folio is opened.
- `/p/[slug]` is a standalone public reader that links externally back to the main domain.

### Cursor Rules

A broad search across the entire `src/` directory found **zero** instances of `cursor: default`, `cursor-default`, or any similar rule that would remove pointer cursors. No suspicious cursor overrides were detected.
